sentence_reviews.text: An agent learns behavior from rewards while interacting with an environment over time.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto book page identifies Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction, 2nd ed.; AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning', including passive/active reinforcement learning, generalization, policy search, applications, and inverse reinforcement learning (lines 555-572).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected AIMA contents support RL topics, and the cited RL book is the canonical source, but the exact definition text was not directly visible in the inspected page.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Game theory & decision theory, Dynamical systems & control theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Reinforcement learning is modeled as depending on Game theory & decision theory, Dynamical systems & control theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Model-based reinforcement learning, Imitation & inverse reinforcement learning, Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
